title: A $2.5m arrears breach triggered fiscal scrutiny and steps to restore creditor confidence.
body_paragraphs: The missed external payment, caused by a cyber theft at the newly established Public Debt Management Office (PDMO), violated a continuous performance criterion (a standing rule tied to program compliance) under a multiyear bailout. Authorities moved to regularise the lapse through a waiver request, noting the shortfall equalled just 0.002% of GDP while agreeing a repayment schedule with the Australian creditor.

The Central Bank submitted its assessment to the Committee on Public Finance (an oversight panel of Parliament) after reviewing the Treasury’s report on the incident. Investigators worked with the Australian Federal Police (AFP, Canberra’s transnational crime agency) as legislators prepared a final report for debate while the International Monetary Fund weighed the waiver against corrective actions.

title: The Government set a timeline to replace its emergency-era anti-terror law within months.
body_paragraphs: Ministers said the Prevention of Terrorism Act (PTA, a 1979 statute used for detention without trial) would be repealed and replaced by a State Prevention of Terrorism Act within about two months. The President also told Parliament the older framework would be abolished in a few months, with the new text drafted to meet international standards.

Officials said the aim was to narrow definitions, tighten judicial oversight and curb administrative detention while safeguarding national security. The Justice Ministry signalled it would table the bill quickly to avoid gaps in the legal architecture once the PTA is struck off the statute book.

title: Parliament advanced the first major overhaul of environmental law in nearly four decades.
body_paragraphs: Lawmakers approved the second reading with 113 votes in favour and 10 against, moving a third amendment to the National Environment Act closer to enactment. It is the most comprehensive rewrite since 1988, when the Environmental Protection Licence and Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA, a pre-project review of ecological effects) were introduced.

Opposition members walked out over devolution clauses tied to provincial responsibilities, but the Government said the package would modernise enforcement and clarify approvals. Officials added that the measure is designed to reduce litigation delays and strengthen compliance in sectors from construction to energy.

title: Health authorities launched a three-day blitz as daily mosquito fever cases topped four figures.
body_paragraphs: Officials reported 1,069 new infections over a single 24‑hour period, bringing the year’s tally to 47,179 and placing 112 Medical Officer of Health divisions on high alert. The worst-hit areas included Maharagama and fast‑spreading clusters in the south, with hospitals reporting more admissions than in the same months of the previous two years.

A nationwide operation ran from 24 to 26 June across 600 high‑risk Grama Niladhari (GN, the smallest civil unit) divisions, combining surveillance, source reduction and enforcement. Authorities said the push was timed to the southwest monsoon, when household containers and clogged drains become key breeding sites.

title: A fleet of training helicopters joined the Air Force under a US transfer.
body_paragraphs: Ten TH-57 Sea Ranger aircraft were handed over at Ratmalana for pilot training and multi‑role tasks such as disaster response and coastal surveillance. The Air Force said the platforms would also support pre‑deployment preparation for United Nations peacekeeping missions, after assembly and test flights to suit local conditions.

title: A lender’s mission began a staff visit to take stock of reforms.
body_paragraphs: An International Monetary Fund (IMF, the global financial safety‑net body) team arrived for meetings from 24 to 30 June to review macro performance and structural changes. Officials said the focus included State‑Owned Enterprise governance, public financial management and adherence to cost‑recovery pricing in energy under the Extended Fund Facility (a multi‑year programme for balance‑of‑payments support).

title: Full enforcement of occupant restraints began nationwide, including on expressways.
body_paragraphs: With the grace period over, police said drivers and front‑seat passengers must buckle up on regular roads and all occupants must do so on high‑speed corridors. The push comes as official data recorded 1,297 road fatalities between January and mid‑June, a toll that policymakers say targeted enforcement can help reduce.

title: A magistrate fined traders for breaching regulated prices on staple grain.
body_paragraphs: Two Dambulla businesses were ordered to pay Rs. 100,000 and Rs. 200,000 after selling Kiri Samba above the gazetted Maximum Retail Price. The Consumer Affairs Authority urged complaints via its 1977 hotline, saying inspectors would intensify checks at wholesale hubs and retail outlets.

title: An appeal court left intact a monk’s jail term over remarks targeting a minority faith.
body_paragraphs: The Colombo High Court rejected the bid to overturn a conviction arising from a 2016 media briefing that a lower court found insulted Islam. The nine months of rigorous imprisonment imposed by the Magistrate’s Court therefore stands, with no change to the sentence.

title: Authorities set a year-end goal for a unified digital land registry.
body_paragraphs: The Agriculture and Lands Ministry said a national digital land information system would be completed this year to streamline deeds, titles and investment approvals. Plans include linking Survey Department mapping and integrating agency databases, with officials saying the upgrade should cut delays and reduce disputes.
